intro: Syneilesis perennis is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Syneilesis perennis is valued for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Syneilesis perennis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Syneilesis perennis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as decoctions and salves.
taxonomy: Syneilesis tagawae is a species of flowering plant in the family Compositae. It is a type of dandelion that is native to East Asia.
